22.reason: [The condition is logically incorrect: it uses && instead of ||, and uses strict inequalities which exclude 'a', 'z', 'A', 'Z'. Also, it doesn't handle non-alphabetic characters correctly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [The condition is logically incorrect: it uses && instead of ||, and uses strict inequalities which exclude 'a', 'z', 'A', 'Z'. Also, it doesn't handle non-alphabetic characters correctly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [The condition is logically incorrect: it uses && between two disjoint ranges, which will never be true. It should use || and include equality (>=, <=) to capture all letters. Also, 'a' < c < 'z' excludes 'a' and 'z'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [The condition is logically incorrect: it uses && instead of ||, and uses strict inequalities which exclude 'a', 'z', 'A', 'Z'. Also, it should check for alphabetic characters using isalpha() or proper inclusive ranges like (c>='a' && c<='z') || (c>='A' && c<='Z').]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Logical error: condition is always false because a character cannot be both between 'a' and 'z' AND between 'A' and 'Z'. Also, the bounds are exclusive (using < instead of <=), so 'a', 'z', 'A', 'Z' are excluded. Should use isalpha() or proper inclusive range checks with ||]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  // The condition is logically incorrect: it uses && instead of ||, and excludes 'a', 'z', 'A', 'Z' due to using strict inequalities. Should be: (c>='a'&&c<='z')||(c>='A'&&c<='Z')

27.reason: [Only handles space and carriage return (13), but not newline (10) or other whitespace/punctuation. This will fail to split words on newlines or punctuation.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Only handles space and carriage return (13), but not newline (10) or other whitespace/punctuation. This will fail to split words on newlines or punctuation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Only handles space and carriage return (13), but not newline (10) or other non-letter characters like punctuation, so words won't be properly separated.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Only handles space and carriage return (13), but not newline ('\n') or other non-alphabetic delimiters like punctuation. This will cause words to be incorrectly split or merged.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Incomplete word delimiter handling: only handles space and carriage return (13), but not newline (10), tab, punctuation, etc. According to problem, any non-letter should delimit a word.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  // Only handles space and carriage return (13), but not newline (10) or other non-letter delimiters like punctuation. This will fail to split words correctly on newlines or punctuation.

38.reason: [Inner loop should iterate over characters of word[i], not over all words (n). This causes out-of-bound access and incorrect tolower usage.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Inner loop should iterate over characters of word[i], not over all words (n). This causes out-of-bound access and incorrect tolower usage.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Inner loop bound should be strlen(word[i]), not n. This writes beyond the word length and may corrupt data.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Inner loop bound should be strlen(word[i]), not n. This causes out-of-bound access and incorrect tolower() application on uninitialized or unrelated memory.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect inner loop bound: should iterate over characters in word[i], not up to n (number of words). This causes out-of-bound access and incorrect tolower application.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  // Inner loop should iterate over characters in word[i], not over n (number of words). Should be: for(j=0; word[i][j]!='\0'; j++)

63.reason: [num array is uninitialized; using num[i]++ leads to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [num array is uninitialized; using num[i]++ leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [num array is uninitialized; using num[i]++ on uninitialized value leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [num array is uninitialized, so incrementing it leads to undefined behavior. Should initialize num to zeros.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Uninitialized array: num[] is not initialized to zero, so incrementing leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  // num array is uninitialized; reading uninitialized values leads to undefined behavior. Should initialize num to zeros.

72.reason: [num[i] is uninitialized for words that never matched duplicates, leading to garbage output.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [num[i] is uninitialized for words that never matched duplicates, leading to garbage output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Because num[i] is uninitialized, num[i]+1 is unpredictable. Also, words of length 0 are skipped, but empty strings may have been inserted incorrectly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Because num[i] was never initialized to 0, num[i]+1 may print garbage values. Also, if a word appears only once, num[i] is uninitialized, not 0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Because num[i] is uninitialized, num[i]+1 is unpredictable. Also, if a word appears only once, num[i] remains garbage, not 0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  // Since num[i] is uninitialized, num[i]+1 may print garbage. Also, if a word appears only once, num[i] is 0 only if initialized, but it's not.
